description.value: The method type of the GenomicStudy analysis. These method types and relevant codes were pulled from [National Library of Medicine-Genetic Testing Registry](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gtr/) (NCBI-GTR) values of describing different testing methods on various levels: [major method category](https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pub/GTR/standard_terms/Major_method_category.txt), [method category](https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pub/GTR/standard_terms/Method_category.txt), and [primary methodology](https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pub/GTR/standard_terms/Primary_test_methodology.txt)

definition.value: Analysis of biochemical compounds and pathways to detect genetic metabolic disorders.
Study of chromosome structure and number using microscopy-based methods.
Analysis of DNA and genes using molecular techniques to identify variations and mutations.
Analysis of biological analytes to detect genetic disorders.
Tests to assess chromosome stability and fragility, often used to detect DNA repair disorders.
Detection of deletions and duplications in DNA sequences.
Identification of homozygous regions in the genome.
Measurement of enzyme activity to detect genetic metabolic disorders.
Fluorescence in situ hybridization performed during interphase of the cell cycle.
Fluorescence in situ hybridization performed during metaphase of the cell cycle.
Analysis of cells using flow cytometry to assess genetic disorders.
Visualization of specific DNA sequences using fluorescent probes.
Detection of antigens in tissue samples using antibodies.
Visual analysis of chromosome structure and number in metaphase cells.
Analysis of genetic markers to identify disease-associated variants in families.
Detection of DNA methylation patterns to assess epigenetic modifications.
Detection of microsatellite instability to assess DNA mismatch repair function.
Multiple fluorescent in situ hybridization using multiple probes and colors simultaneously.
Screening for mutations in specific exons of targeted genes.

display.value: Mutation scanning of the entire coding region

definition.value: Comprehensive screening for mutations throughout the entire coding region of a gene.
Analysis of protein structure and function to detect genetic disorders affecting protein.
Measurement of protein expression levels to assess genetic effects on protein production.
Analysis of RNA sequences and expression to detect genetic disorders.
DNA sequencing of specific exons to identify mutations.

display.value: Sequence analysis of the entire coding region

definition.value: Comprehensive DNA sequencing of the entire coding region of a gene.
Assessment of sister chromatid exchange as an indicator of DNA damage and repair.
Sequencing or genotyping of specific, pre-selected genetic variants.
Detection of regions where both chromosomes are inherited from a single parent.
Genotyping method using allele-specific primers to identify genetic variants.
Detection and analysis of alternative splicing patterns in RNA.
DNA sequencing using Sanger method in both forward and reverse directions.
Chromosome banding technique visualizing constitutive heterochromatin.
Immunoassay detecting antigens using chemiluminescent reaction.
ChIP-on-chip analysis combining chromatin immunoprecipitation with microarray.
Detection of copy number variations by comparison of test and control DNA.
DNA Adenine Methyltransferase Identification for mapping protein-DNA interactions.
Computer-based analysis of chromosome patterns and abnormalities.
Microfluidic technology using digital control for genetic analysis.
Measurement of enzyme levels in biological samples.
Measurement of enzyme activity levels in biological samples.
Immunoassay for detecting antigens using enzyme-linked antibodies.
Measurement of fluorescence intensity to quantify biological molecules.
Microarray-based detection of gene fusion events.
Giemsa staining chromosome banding technique visualizing chromosome structure.
Technique combining gas chromatography with mass spectrometry for chemical compound separation and identification.
Analysis of gene expression patterns using various molecular techniques.
Identification of genes through sequence comparison or database lookup.
Molecular detection technique using gold nanoparticles as probes.
Separation and analysis technique using high-pressure liquid chromatography.
Combined technique using liquid chromatography followed by mass spectrometry.
Laboratory technique using liquid chromatography with tandem mass spectrometry.
Measurement of metabolite concentrations in biological samples.
PCR-based technique detecting DNA methylation patterns at specific loci.
Molecular technique using arrays of probes to detect DNA sequences or expression levels.
Technique for detecting copy number variations and point mutations.
High-throughput sequencing technology for analyzing genetic variation.
DNA sequence detection method using complementary oligonucleotide ligation.
DNA sequencing using hybridization with oligonucleotide probes.
Other genetic testing method not listed in this code system.
Polymerase Chain Reaction for amplifying and detecting DNA sequences.
PCR technique combined with allele-specific probe hybridization for variant detection.
Combination of PCR-RFLP and Southern blot analysis for genetic variation detection.
Molecular technique detecting premature stop codons and truncated proteins.
DNA sequencing method based on detection of pyrophosphate release during nucleotide incorporation.
Quinacrine staining chromosome banding technique using fluorescent dye.
Real-time PCR technique for quantifying DNA or RNA sequences.
Reverse chromosome banding technique visualizing reverse of normal G-banding pattern.
Restriction Fragment Length Polymorphism - technique detecting DNA variations via restriction mapping.
Reverse transcription Loop-mediated Isothermal Amplification for RNA detection.
Reverse transcription PCR for detecting and amplifying RNA sequences.
RT-PCR product analysis using gel electrophoresis for visualization and sizing.
Real-time reverse transcription PCR for quantitative RNA detection.
Detection of single nucleotide polymorphisms through molecular testing.
Staining technique used in protein and DNA analysis for visualization.
Fluorescence in situ hybridization allowing simultaneous visualization of all chromosomes.
T-banding chromosome technique visualizing telomeres through staining.
Tandem mass spectrometry technique for analyzing molecular structure and mass.

display.value: Tetra-nucleotide repeat by PCR or Southern Blot

definition.value: Detection of tetra-nucleotide repeat expansions using PCR or Southern blot analysis.
Microarray technology using overlapping probes to analyze genomic regions.

display.value: Trinucleotide repeat by PCR or Southern Blot

definition.value: Detection of trinucleotide repeat expansions using PCR or Southern blot analysis.
One-directional Sanger sequencing method for DNA sequence determination.
